subject: Re: prelife
body:
it reduces the complication s of self organizing . it should be looked at as a phase transition, chaos to life to dust ( just kidding) pre life life death, just three phases ice water gas. gas rising to heaven

----- Original Message ----
From: Martin Nowak <nowak@fas.harvard.edu>
To: J. Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Sent: Monday, March 24, 2008 7:14:26 AM
Subject: prelife

dear jeffrey,

i can certainly do this.
it is a great idea.
the temperature affects the mutation rate.
if mutation is too high then life does not 'crystallize'.
lowering the mutation rate makes life 'precipitate' from a saturated  
solution.
it is a great concept. i love it.

i can tell you the other developments.
i am now revising the prelife paper.
(i am at home 781 259 4297.)

best wishes
martin





On Mar 24, 2008, at 7:40 AM, J. Epstein wrote:

with our pre-life,, I think as opposed to a bottom contruction, why  
don't we try life as a precipitate from a super saturated solution. .  
instead of a miller like spark, we get a phase transition, when the  
life soup finally cools

----- Original Message ----
From: Martin Nowak <nowak@fas.harvard.edu>
To: jeeproject@yahoo.com
Sent: Thursday, March 20, 2008 1:46:38 PM
Subject: proposal


dear jeffrey

please find attached the proposal.
i hope you like it.
please also find attached ped's publication list and mine.

one other thing:
the olympic champion is also the world champion!

best wishes
martin
